summary:
Diarrhea is not a recognized or expected symptom of congenital hypothyoidism resulting from maternal iodine deficiency; typically, gastrointestinal issues present as constipation, not diarrhea. This makes diarrhea inconsistent with established clinical findings for this condition.  
$\boxed{\text{B}}$

This makes diarrhea inconsistent with established clinical findings for this condition.  
$\boxed{\text{B}}$. Answer choice: B
